title: Type 1 DM New Diagnosis with DKA - Adolescent

patient_data.chief_complaint: Excessive thirst, frequent urination, weight loss, and progressive lethargy over 3 weeks
symptoms: Polyuria — waking 4-5 times at night to urinate
Polydipsia — drinking 5-6 liters of water daily
Unintentional weight loss of 8 kg over 3 weeks
Progressive fatigue and difficulty concentrating in school
Abdominal pain and nausea since yesterday
patient_data.history: Previously healthy university student, no significant PMH, family history: mother with Hashimoto's thyroiditis

lab_results.potassium: 5.8 mEq/L (high — but will drop with insulin)

expected_output.primary_diagnosis: New-onset Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus with Diabetic Ketoacidosis
should_detect: DKA protocol: IV normal saline bolus then 250-500 mL/hr
Regular insulin infusion 0.1 U/kg/hr (NOT bolus in new diagnosis)
Potassium monitoring q2h — DO NOT give insulin if K+ < 3.3
Current K+ 5.8 will drop rapidly with insulin — monitor closely
Correct sodium for glucose: corrected Na ≈ 134
Bridge to subcutaneous insulin when anion gap closes and patient eating
Diabetes education: carb counting, insulin therapy, DKA prevention
Autoimmune panel: GAD65+ confirms Type 1 (not Type 2)
Screen for other autoimmune conditions (thyroid — maternal history)
red_flags: Cerebral edema risk in young patients
